compare_page.title: Florida PFAS filters after a utility report: certified point-of-use options
compare_page.lede: This compare page is for Florida households that already read a seeded utility dossier and now need a certified point-of-use shortlist without flattening notice-heavy and CCR-led systems into one state answer.
compare_page.nextActionTitle: Keep the compare lane tied to the exact Florida utility dossier
compare_page.nextActionSummary: Use Florida as a navigation layer, but keep the shortlist anchored to the exact utility record, its timing, and the current system interpretation before comparing certified point-of-use hardware.
comparisonAxes: Claim-level PFAS support for an ingestion-focused route
Whether the product stays proportionate to a Florida utility dossier
whyThis: Florida now has multiple seeded utility dossiers with different PFAS posture.
Households searching within Florida still need a system-led compare lane rather than a statewide roundup.
This page gives the project a narrower commercial lane than a generic best-filter page.
whatThisDoesNotTellYou: This page does not replace the utility dossier.
It does not turn one Florida record into a fit for another system.
It does not justify whole-house treatment.
evidenceHighlights: Florida now includes both notice-heavy Sunshine Water routes and quieter CCR-led utility dossiers.
EPA filter guidance still keeps claim review tied to exact certified products.
A Florida compare lane is strongest when it stays downstream from the system record.
targetQueries: florida pfas filter after utility report
florida pfas filter after water notice

sections.title: Why Florida needs its own post-utility compare lane
sections.summary: A regional compare lane is useful only if it stays downstream from the exact system record instead of pretending Florida is one PFAS document.
paragraphs: The Florida cluster now includes multiple public-water dossiers with different route posture. That makes a regional compare page useful because it can answer the buying query without erasing the fact that the household still belongs to one exact system.
This page exists to stop the state label from becoming lazy commercial shorthand. The system record still leads.
bullets: The exact utility dossier is the evidence layer.
Certified point-of-use is the commercial layer.

sections.title: What the shortlist should reward
sections.summary: The strongest shortlist keeps maintenance and claim fit visible while blocking whole-house drift.
paragraphs: A Florida household coming out of a utility dossier usually needs a tighter point-of-use decision, not a broader prestige ladder. That means the page should reward claim scope, installation realism, and annual ownership burden before anything else.
Products that stay proportionate to an ingestion-focused public-water route should remain on top. Bigger and louder hardware should still need separate justification.

sections.title: How this protects both trust and conversion quality
sections.summary: It gives Florida users a buying step without rewriting the record they just read.
paragraphs: A statewide buying query often hides a much better intent: 'I already saw a utility record and now need a narrower next step.' This page is built for that exact moment.
By holding the system dossier and the point-of-use shortlist in the same frame, the page supports cleaner clicks and a more defensible commercial path.
bullets: It answers a commercial query without skipping interpretation.
It improves click quality by narrowing the path.
It keeps Florida from collapsing into location-spam commerce.
